heading: Program for eliminating duplication of environmental reviews
label: 330 Program for eliminating duplication of environmental reviews

A, title I, § 1309(a)129 Stat. 1392Dec. 4, 2015“The purpose of this section [enacting this section and provisions set out as a note under this section] is to eliminate duplication of environmental reviews and approvals under State laws and the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).”42 U.S.C. 4321

Rulemaking, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–94, div. A, title I, § 1309(c)129 Stat. 1396Dec. 4, 2015“(1).—In generalNot later than 270 days after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Secretary [of Transportation], in consultation with the Chair of the Council on Environmental Quality, shall promulgate regulations to implement the requirements of , United States Code, as added by this section.Dec. 4, 2015section 330 of title 23“(2).—Determination of stringencyAs part of the rulemaking required under this subsection, the Chair shall—“(A)establish the criteria necessary to determine that a State law or regulation is at least as stringent as a Federal requirement described in , United States Code; andsection 330(a)(3) of title 23“(B)ensure that the criteria, at a minimum—“(i)provide for protection of the environment;“(ii)provide opportunity for public participation and comment, including access to the documentation necessary to review the potential impact of a project; and“(iii)ensure a consistent review of projects that would otherwise have been covered under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).”42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a pilot program to authorize States that have assumed responsibilities of the Secretary under section 327 and are approved to participate in the program under this section to conduct environmental reviews and make approvals for projects under State environmental laws and regulations instead of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.), consistent with the requirements of this section.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: .—Participating states
children.label: 2 .—Participating states
children.text: The Secretary may select not more than 2 States to participate in the program.

children.heading: .—Alternative environmental review and approval procedures defined
children.label: 3 .—Alternative environmental review and approval procedures defined
i
children.text: the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: ii
children.text: any provisions of section 139 establishing procedures for the implementation of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) that are under the authority of the Secretary, as the Secretary, in consultation with the State, considers appropriate; and42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: iii
children.text: related regulations and Executive orders; and

children.label: i
children.text: the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: ii
children.text: any provisions of section 139 establishing procedures for the implementation of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) that are under the authority of the Secretary, as the Secretary, in consultation with the State, considers appropriate; and42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: iii
children.text: related regulations and Executive orders.

children.label: A
children.text: the procedures the State uses to engage the public and consider alternatives to the proposed action; and

children.label: B
children.text: the extent to which the State considers environmental consequences or impacts on resources potentially impacted by the proposed action (such as air, water, or species);
each Federal requirement described in subsection (a)(3) that the State is seeking to substitute;
each State law or regulation that the State intends to substitute for such Federal requirement;
an explanation of the basis for concluding that the State law or regulation is at least as stringent as the Federal requirement described in subsection (a)(3);
a description of the projects or classes of projects for which the State anticipates exercising the authority that may be granted under the program;
verification that the State has the financial resources necessary to carry out the authority that may be granted under the program;
evidence of having sought, received, and addressed comments on the proposed application from the public; and
any such additional information as the Secretary, or, with respect to section (d)(1)(A), the Secretary in consultation with the Chair, may require.
review and accept public comments on an application submitted under subsection (b);
approve or disapprove the application not later than 120 days after the date of receipt of an application that the Secretary determines is complete; and
transmit to the State notice of the approval or disapproval, together with a statement of the reasons for the approval or disapproval.

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary, with the concurrence of the Chair and after considering any public comments received pursuant to subsection (c), determines that the laws and regulations of the State described in the application are at least as stringent as the Federal requirements described in subsection (a)(3);

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary, after considering any public comments received pursuant to subsection (c), determines that the State has the capacity, including financial and personnel, to assume the responsibility;

children.label: C
children.text: the State has executed an agreement with the Secretary in accordance with section 327; and

children.label: i
children.text: has been executed by the Governor or the top-ranking transportation official in the State who is charged with responsibility for highway construction;

children.label: ii
children.text: is in such form as the Secretary may prescribe;

children.label: I
children.text: agrees to assume the responsibilities, as identified by the Secretary, under this section;

children.label: II
children.text: expressly consents, on behalf of the State, to accept the jurisdiction of the Federal courts under subsection (e)(1) for the compliance, discharge, and enforcement of any responsibility under this section;

children.label: aa
children.text: authorize the State to take the actions necessary to carry out the responsibilities being assumed; and

children.label: bb
children.text: are comparable to , including providing that any decision regarding the public availability of a document under those State laws is reviewable by a court of competent jurisdiction; andsection 552 of title 5

children.label: IV
children.text: agrees to maintain the financial resources necessary to carry out the responsibilities being assumed;

children.label: iv
children.text: requires the State to provide to the Secretary any information the Secretary reasonably considers necessary to ensure that the State is adequately carrying out the responsibilities assigned to the State;

children.label: v
children.text: has a term of not more than 5 years; and

children.label: vi
children.text: is renewable.

children.heading: .—Exclusion
children.label: 2 .—Exclusion
children.text: The National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) shall not apply to a decision by the Secretary to approve or disapprove an application submitted under this section.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: A
children.text: to meet the requirements of this section; or

children.label: B
children.text: to follow the alternative environmental review and approval procedures approved pursuant to this section.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a claim seeking judicial review of a permit, license, or approval issued by a State under this section shall be barred unless the claim is filed not later than 150 days as set forth in section 139() after the date of publication in the Federal Register by the Secretary of a notice that the permit, license, or approval is final pursuant to the law under which the action is taken.l

children.heading: .—Notification
children.label: i .—Notification
children.text: The State shall notify the Secretary of the final action of the State not later than 10 days after the final action is taken.

children.heading: .—Publication
children.label: ii .—Publication
children.text: The Secretary shall publish the notice of final action in the Federal Register not later than 30 days after the date of receipt of the notice under clause (i).

children.heading: .—Savings provision
children.label: C .—Savings provision
children.text: Nothing in this subsection creates a right to judicial review or places any limit on filing a claim that a person has violated the terms of a permit, license, or approval.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: A State shall consider new information received after the close of a comment period if the information satisfies the requirements for a supplemental environmental impact statement under section 771.130 of title 23, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations).

children.heading: Treatment of final agency action.—
children.label: B Treatment of final agency action.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: The final agency action that follows preparation of a supplemental environmental impact statement, if required, shall be considered a separate final agency action, and the deadline for filing a claim for judicial review of the action shall be 150 days as set forth in section 139() after the date of publication in the Federal Register by the Secretary of a notice announcing such action.l

children.heading: .—Notification
children.label: I .—Notification
children.text: The State shall notify the Secretary of the final action of the State not later than 10 days after the final action is taken.

children.heading: .—Publication
children.label: II .—Publication
children.text: The Secretary shall publish the notice of final action in the Federal Register not later than 30 days after the date of receipt of the notice under subclause (I).

children.heading: .—Election
children.label: f .—Election
children.text: A State participating in the programs under this section and section 327, at the discretion of the State, may elect to apply the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) instead of the alternative environmental review and approval procedures of the State.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: .—Adoption or Incorporation by Reference of Documents
children.label: g .—Adoption or Incorporation by Reference of Documents
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable and consistent with Federal law, other Federal agencies with authority over a project subject to this section shall adopt or incorporate by reference documents produced by a participating State under this section to satisfy the requirements of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: Relationship to Locally Administered Projects.—
children.label: h Relationship to Locally Administered Projects.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: A State with an approved program under this section, at the request of a local government, may exercise authority under that program on behalf of up to 25 local governments for locally administered projects.

children.heading: .—Scope
children.label: 2 .—Scope
children.text: For up to 25 local governments selected by a State with an approved program under this section, the State shall be responsible for ensuring that any environmental review, consultation, or other action required under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) or the State program, or both, meets the requirements of such Act or program.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: A State program approved under this section shall at all times be in accordance with the requirements of this section.

children.heading: .—Review
children.label: 2 .—Review
children.text: The Secretary shall review each State program approved under this section not less than once every 5 years.

children.heading: .—Public notice and comment
children.label: 3 .—Public notice and comment
children.text: In conducting the review process under paragraph (2), the Secretary shall provide notice and an opportunity for public comment.

children.heading: .—Withdrawal of approval
children.label: 4 .—Withdrawal of approval
children.text: If the Secretary, in consultation with the Chair, determines at any time that a State is not administering a State program approved under this section in accordance with the requirements of this section, the Secretary shall so notify the State, and if appropriate corrective action is not taken within a reasonable time, not to exceed 90 days, the Secretary shall withdraw approval of the State program.

children.heading: .—Extensions and terminations
children.label: 5 .—Extensions and terminations
children.text: At the conclusion of the review process under paragraph (2), the Secretary may extend for an additional 5-year period or terminate the authority of a State under this section to substitute the laws and regulations of the State for the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 4321
the number of States participating in the program;
the number and types of projects for which each State participating in the program has used alternative environmental review and approval procedures;
a description and assessment of whether implementation of the program has resulted in more efficient review of projects; and
any recommendations for modifications to the program.

children.heading: .—Sunset
children.label: k .—Sunset
children.text: The program shall terminate 12 years after the date of enactment of this section.

children.heading: .—Chair
children.label: 1 .—Chair
children.text: The term “Chair” means the Chair of the Council on Environmental Quality.

children.heading: .—Multimodal project
children.label: 2 .—Multimodal project
children.text: The term “multimodal project” has the meaning given that term in section 139(a).

children.heading: .—Program
children.label: 3 .—Program
children.text: The term “program” means the pilot program established under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: a project requiring approval under this title, chapter 53 of subtitle III of title 49, or subtitle V of title 49; and

children.label: B
children.text: a multimodal project.
